name: sandbox
notes: T1 sandbox, plain (no sensitive_data). Dual-runtime by explicit owner request: both Claude and Codex run sessions here. The deny floor is NOT vendored into this repo (no .claude/hooks/ copy) to avoid the global+repo double-spawn tracked as Taskdeck #1293. Claude gets the floor from the GLOBAL PreToolUse hook (~/.claude/hooks/dispatch.py, floor v1.4.1, read via CLAUDE_PROJECT_DIR). Codex gets the SAME dispatcher via .codex/hooks.json with --runtime codex; .codex/config.toml enables hooks and disables multi-agent fan-out at T1. Only the PRE deny event is wired at T1 — no ledger/session/stop machinery. Codex project hooks are trust-gated and must be reviewed via /hooks after their definition changes. bypassPermissions lives in gitignored .claude/settings.local.json (owner wants max trust by default); committed settings.json keeps acceptEdits + the floor deny list as the fresh-clone baseline. human_todo=null: T1 has no human-action file. M0 foundation is implemented and on origin/main; Study 001 remains draft, no real provider or paid LLM run is enabled, and human-owned decisions remain in HANDOFF.md. The repo's 3rd-return-session promotion signal now appears met, but T1 is retained until the human reviews and declares a T2 promotion. If real human-subject or participant data ever enters, flip sensitive_data BEFORE it lands. Bootstrapped 2026-07-12 per agent-harness/MIGRATION_PROMPT.md 'Any NEW repo' recipe, mirroring the EvidenceDeck dual-runtime reference.
